fact_text: Hooded seals are named for the bi-lobed hood, an enlargement of the nasal cavity on the heads of males, which can be inflated

Walrus tusks are actually enlarged canine teeth that never stop growing
Walruses have over 400 whiskers
Fur seals are not 'true seals'
You can tell a true seal apart from a seal lion by their ears. True seals do not have visible external ear flaps
Seals move on land by galumphing

Antarctic fur seals rely on a thick double-coat of fur instead of blubber to stay warm

Stellar sea lions have been nicknamed the 'Grizzly of the Sea'

Stellar sea lions eat about 6% of their body weight in fish a day

The Japanese sea lion is extinct
